data.title: Replimune Group GAAP EPS of -$3.07 misses by $0.01
data.description:
• Replimune Group press release (NASDAQ:REPL): FY GAAP EPS of -$3.07 misses by $0.01.
  • As of March 31, 2025, cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments were $483.8 million, as compared to $420.7 million as of fiscal year ended March 31, 2024. The increase in cash balance was a result of the public offering in November 2024, somewhat offset by cash utilized in operating activities in advancing the Company’s clinical development plans.
  • Based on the current operating plan, the Company believes that existing cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments, as of March 31, 2025 will enable the Company to fund operations into the fourth quarter of 2026 which includes scale up for the potential commercialization of RP1 in skin cancers and for working capital and general corporate purposes and excludes any potential revenue.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Advance Auto Parts reports strong Q1, outlook
CSW Industrials Non-GAAP EPS of $2.24 beats by $0.02, revenue of $230.55M misses by $2.27M
data.description:
• CSW Industrials press release (NASDAQ:CSWI): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.24 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $230.55M (+9.3% Y/Y) misses by $2.27M.
  • Total revenue increased 9.3% to a fourth quarter record of $230.5 million, driven by inorganic growth of 6.3% from the recent acquisitions of Dust Free, PSP Products, and PF WaterWorks, and organic growth of 3.0%

data.title: Advance Auto Parts soars after better-than-feared Q1 report, strong profit guidance
data.description:
Advance Auto Parts (NYSE:AAP) soared in early trading on Thursday after topping revenue and EPS estimates with its Q1 earnings report. During the quarter, Advance Auto Parts (NYSE:AAP) also successfully completed its store footprint optimization within an accelerated timeframe, while continuing to make progress on our other strategic initiatives. 

Comparable store sales for the quarter decreased 0.6% vs. -2.0% consensus. That tally does not include store closing sales at more than 500 corporate locations that were closed in the quarter as part of Advance Auto Parts' (AAP) store optimization program. Total revenue was down 6.9% year-over-year during the quarter.

Advance Auto Parts' (AAP) gross profit rate for the quarter was down 40 basis points to 42.9% of sales. The deleveraging was primarily driven by approximately 90 basis points of margin headwind associated with liquidation sales at closing store locations. AAP recorded an adjusted operating loss of $8 vs. adjusted operating income of $56 million for the same quarter a year ago.

Free cash flow through Q1 of 2025 was an outflow of $198 million, compared with an outflow of $49 million in the same period of the prior year. 

The auto retailer reaffirmed FY25 guidance for revenue of $8.4 billion to $8.6 billion (midpoint $8.5 billion) vs. $8.47 consensus and EPS of $1.50 to $2.50 (midpoint $2.00) vs. $1.42 consensus/

"The recently implemented tariffs have created a highly dynamic economic environment. Despite this, the team is staying focused on the turnaround and our path ahead," highlighted CEO Shane O'Kelly. "We are reaffirming our annual guidance based on performance to date, expected progress on our strategic initiatives for the balance of the year and our planned mitigation actions for the tariffs currently in effect," added O'Kelly.

Shares of Advance Auto Parts (AAP) jumped 14.9% in premarket action after shedding more than 10% in the week ahead of the report. Short interest on AAP stands at 17.4% of the total float.

data.title: Kanzhun Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.24 beats by $0.02, revenue of $265M misses by $0.5M
data.description:
• Kanzhun press release (NASDAQ:BZ): Q1 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.24 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $265M (+12.9% Y/Y) misses by $0.5M.
  • Revenues for the first quarter of 2025 were RMB1,923.3 million (US$265.0 million), an increase of 12.9% from RMB1,703.8 million for the same quarter of 2024.
  • Total paid enterprise customers in the twelve months ended March 31, 2025 were 6.4 million, an increase of 12.3% from 5.7 million in the twelve months ended March 31, 2024.
  • Average monthly active users for the first quarter of 2025 were 57.6 million, an increase of 23.6% from 46.6 million for the same quarter of 2024.
  • For the second quarter of 2025, the Company currently expects its total revenues to be between RMB2.05 billion and RMB2.08 billion, representing a year-on-year increase of 6.9% to 8.5%.

data.title: Thermon Non-GAAP EPS of $0.56 beats by $0.05, revenue of $134.1M beats by $0.45M
data.description:
• Thermon press release (NYSE:THR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.56 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $134.1M (+5.0% Y/Y) beats by $0.45M.
  • Fourth quarter revenue was $134.1 million, an increase of 5% compared to same period last year, driven by continued momentum in OPEX revenues and contribution from F.A.T.I., partially offset by softness in large project revenue. Excluding revenue contributed from F.A.T.I., fourth quarter organic revenue increased 3%.

  • New orders of $138.8 million, an increase of 19%; book-to-bill ratio of 1.04x

  • Net leverage ratio of 0.9x as of March 31, 2025

  • FY25 revenue consensus of $497.76M, EPS consensus of $1.83.

  • The following forward-looking guidance reflects the Company's current expectations and beliefs for fiscal 2026 as of May 22, 2025, and is subject to change.

data.title: Elbit Systems prices offering to raise $512M
data.description:
• Defense technology firm Elbit Systems (NASDAQ:ESLT) has priced an offering of 1,365,450 ordinary shares at $375 per share to raise approximately $512M in proceeds.
  • The underwriters have been granted a 30-day option to purchase 204,817 additional ordinary shares at the public offering price, less underwriting discounts and commissions.
  • The ordinary shares offered in this offering are expected to be listed for trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market.
  • The offering is expected to close on May 23, 2025, subject to customary closing conditions.
  • Elbit Systems intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for general corporate purposes.
  • Elbit Systems (NASDAQ:ESLT) shares fell 3.99% to $380.14 premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Sonae, SGPS, S.A. reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Sonae, SGPS, S.A. (OTCPK:SOSSF): Q1 Net result rose to €43m, up 77% yoy, reflecting improved operational performance across our portfolio companies which offset the impact of higher D&A and financial costs associated with portfolio expansion.
  • Revenue of $2.55B (+22.6% Y/Y).

data.title: MPC Container Ships ASA Non-GAAP EPS of $0.11
data.description:
• MPC Container Ships ASA press release (OTCPK:MPZZF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.11.
  • Operating revenues of USD 127.1 million (Q1 2024: USD 147.5 million) and EBITDA of USD 77.8 million (Q1 2024: USD 96.1 million). EBITDA adjusted for non-recurring items was USD 66.2 million (Q1 2024: USD 96.3 million)
  • Charter backlog of USD 1.1 billion with almost full contract coverage for 2025 (96%) and high coverage for 2026 (77%)
  • Quarterly recurring dividend of USD 0.08 per share, bringing the total dividends to over USD 1 billion declared since February 2022
  • Reaffirmed guidance for 2025 of operating revenues in the range of USD 485-500 million and EBITDA in the range of USD 305-325 million
